name: Where in my page code should the generated JSON-LD actually go, especially on dynamic or JS-heavy sites?
description: Place generated JSON-LD in one or a few consolidated `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks in the head or near the end of the body. The key is that it’s

mainEntity.name: Where in my page code should the generated JSON-LD actually go, especially on dynamic or JS-heavy sites?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Most of your per-page JSON-LD should live in the `<head>` or near the end of the `<body>`, generated server‑side or by your CMS. Having dozens of small script blocks scattered throughout dynamic components is harder to maintain and debug.

From a search engine perspective, placement in head vs. body usually doesn’t matter as long as the markup is valid and rendered on initial load.

KEY FACTORS
- Keep schema centralized per page (one or a few script blocks, not many tiny ones).
- Avoid blocking or delaying schema with heavy client‑side JS.
- Ensure markup is rendered for bots (SSR or prerendering if needed).
- Consistent placement per template simplifies QA.

Ask your dev team to wire schema generation into your existing layout templates rather than component‑by‑component. That keeps things predictable.

A generator that outputs consolidated, page‑level JSON-LD scripts is generally best for maintainability.
acceptedAnswer.description: Place generated JSON-LD in one or a few consolidated `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks in the head or near the end of the body. The key is that it’s valid, rendered for bots on load, and managed at the page/template level for easier maintenance.
